mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate declines below 1% signal monetary accommodation that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle with rates near zero lower bound
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS < 1.0%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate above 7% signals labor market slack and economic weakness that constrains corporate profit growth and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as growth expectations compress.
regime_hint: post-shock recovery or weak labor market regime
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of periods when UNRATE > 7%, or correlation between UNRATE and forward returns is positive and statistically significant (p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts below 900 thousand units annualized indicate weakening residential investment and forward demand signals, reducing near-term economic momentum and equity market returns over 5 trading days as forward guidance deteriorates.
evidence_refs: fact:current HOUST=917.0 thousand units near threshold
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 65% of instances when HOUST < 900, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index declining below 99.0 signals supply-side weakness and capacity underutilization that reduces profit margins and equity valuations. This mechanism is amplified when combined with rising unemployment, indicating demand-supply mismatch.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 58% of triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient between INDPRO and UNRATE is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in predictive regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth measured by RSAFS combined with housing starts growth indicates broad-based consumer and residential demand strength that supports equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days when both series show month-over-month expansion.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS=418284 near threshold, HOUST=917.0 near threshold
regime_hint: demand-driven expansion with synchronized consumer and housing activity
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or correlation between joint RSAFS-HOUST strength and forward returns is negative or insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth measured by PAYEMS above 135 million persons signals robust labor market momentum and income growth that supports consumer spending and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:current PAYEMS=135195 thousand persons indicates strong employment
regime_hint: labor market expansion or late-cycle employment strength
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 55% of periods when PAYEMS > 135000, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL above 235 combined with federal funds rate below 1% signals real negative rates that erode fixed-income returns and redirect capital to equities, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as real rate compression accelerates.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL=232.77 near threshold, FEDFUNDS=0.14% deeply negative in real terms
regime_hint: monetary accommodation with elevated inflation regime
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 52% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect between inflation and low rates is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in predictive models.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth measured by GDPC1 below 13700 billion chained 2017 dollars signals economic deceleration and below-trend growth that compresses equity earnings multiples and produces negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as recession risk increases.
regime_hint: late-cycle deceleration or early-stage recession regime
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of instances when GDPC1 < 13700, or correlation between GDP growth and forward returns is negative and statistically significant (p < 0.05).
